title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 40 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Norway's primary national holiday marking the start of the new year with celebrations across all regions.

festivals.name: Sami National Day
festivals.description: Celebrates Sami culture and heritage with traditional dress, music, and cultural events throughout northern Norway.

festivals.name: Lillehammer Winter Festival
festivals.description: Winter sports competitions and cultural celebrations in the Olympic city featuring skiing, ice skating, and Nordic traditions.

festivals.name: Holmenkollen Ski Festival
festivals.description: One of the world's oldest ski competitions held in Oslo, attracting international athletes and thousands of spectators for Nordic skiing events.

festivals.name: Mawkyrwat Folk Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of traditional Norwegian folk music, dance, and crafts with performances from regional artists.

festivals.name: Grünerløkka Street Festival
festivals.description: Oslo's quirky and colorful street festival celebrating alternative culture with live music, food, and artistic performances.

festivals.name: Maundy Thursday
festivals.description: A public holiday marking the Thursday before Easter with traditional processions and religious observances across Norway.

festivals.name: Good Friday
festivals.description: A national public holiday commemorating the crucifixion of Jesus with church services and family gatherings throughout Norway.

festivals.name: Easter Sunday
festivals.description: Norway's major religious holiday celebrating the resurrection of Jesus with church services, family meals, and egg hunts.

festivals.name: Easter Monday
festivals.description: A public holiday following Easter Sunday with continued family celebrations and outdoor activities across the country.

festivals.name: Bergenfest
festivals.description: Bergen's premier music and culture festival featuring international and Norwegian artists across multiple genres and venues.

festivals.name: Labour Day
festivals.description: A national public holiday celebrating workers' rights with parades, speeches, and gatherings across all Norwegian cities.

festivals.name: Norwegian Constitution Day
festivals.description: Norway's most important national holiday celebrating independence with parades, traditional costumes, and festivities in every town and village.

festivals.name: Ascension Day
festivals.description: A public holiday celebrated 40 days after Easter, marking the ascension of Jesus with religious services and family time.

festivals.name: Whit Sunday
festivals.description: A public holiday 50 days after Easter celebrating the descent of the Holy Spirit with church services and family gatherings.

festivals.name: Whit Monday
festivals.description: A public holiday following Whit Sunday with continued celebrations and outdoor recreational activities across Norway.

festivals.name: Midnight Sun Marathon
festivals.description: A unique marathon run under the midnight sun in Tromsø, combining sport with the extraordinary Arctic natural phenomenon.

festivals.name: St. John's Eve (Midsummer)
festivals.description: Norway's most beloved summer celebration featuring bonfires, folk music, traditional costumes, and outdoor festivities marking the summer solstice.

festivals.name: Øya Festival
festivals.description: An ecologically conscious music and culture festival near Oslo featuring international acts and sustainability focus.

festivals.name: Roskilde Festival (nearby influence)
festivals.description: While in Denmark, this major Nordic festival draws many Norwegian visitors for world-class music and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Notodden Blues Festival
festivals.description: One of Europe's largest blues festivals featuring international blues artists and attracting music enthusiasts from across the region.

festivals.name: Øl Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Norwegian craft beer culture featuring local breweries, tastings, and food pairings in Bergen.

festivals.name: Rjukan Notodden Blues Festival
festivals.description: Central Norway's premier blues music festival attracting international performers and thousands of music lovers.

festivals.name: Bergen International Film Festival
festivals.description: One of Scandinavia's largest film festivals showcasing international cinema and documentaries across Bergen's venues.

festivals.name: Stavanger Food Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Norwegian coastal cuisine featuring seafood, local ingredients, and culinary demonstrations by renowned chefs.

festivals.name: Northern Lights Festival Tromsø
festivals.description: A celebration of Arctic culture and the aurora borealis season featuring music, theater, and Northern Lights viewing opportunities.

festivals.name: All Saints' Day
festivals.description: A public holiday and day of remembrance for the deceased celebrated with cemetery visits and family gatherings.

festivals.name: Oslo Jazz Festival
festivals.description: Norway's leading jazz festival featuring world-class international and Norwegian jazz artists in multiple venues across Oslo.

festivals.name: Christmas Season Markets
festivals.description: Traditional Christmas markets throughout Norway featuring festive decorations, local crafts, mulled wine, and seasonal treats.

festivals.name: Christmas Eve
festivals.description: Norway's main Christmas celebration with family gatherings, traditional meals, gift-giving, and church services throughout the country.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: A national public holiday continuing Christmas celebrations with family time, church services, and traditional Norwegian Christmas traditions.

festivals.name: Boxing Day
festivals.description: A public holiday following Christmas marked by continued family celebrations and outdoor winter activities across Norway.

festivals.name: New Year's Eve
festivals.description: A major celebration across Norway with parties, fireworks displays, and public gatherings welcoming the new year.

festivals.name: Lillehammer Film Festival
festivals.description: An international film festival showcasing documentaries and feature films in the Olympic city of Lillehammer.

festivals.name: Arctic Race of Norway
festivals.description: A professional cycling race spanning Arctic regions featuring stages through dramatic Norwegian landscapes and Arctic communities.

festivals.name: Namdalseid Jazz Festival
festivals.description: A boutique jazz festival in central Norway featuring international musicians and intimate performances in scenic settings.

festivals.name: Hardanger Bridge Walk
festivals.description: A unique festival celebrating the Hardanger Bridge with walking events, cultural performances, and exhibitions.

festivals.name: Ibsen Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Norway's greatest playwright Henrik Ibsen featuring theatrical performances, exhibitions, and cultural discussions.

festivals.name: Åre Festival
festivals.description: A summer music and culture festival in the Alpine resort town of Åre featuring diverse entertainment and mountain scenery.

festivals.name: Vossa Jazz Festival
festivals.description: A premier jazz festival in the fjord region attracting world-renowned musicians and featuring scenic outdoor performances.
